Regency at Troy

Overview

Address
2685 West Maple Road, Troy, MI 48084
Phone
2489657900
Certified beds
154
Avg daily residents
114 (74% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit (individual owner)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2024-04-19
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.31 3.75 4.44 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.63 0.66 0.65 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.44 0.83 1.49 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.23 2.26 2.30 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.07 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.13 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.62
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.30
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.73
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.33
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-27 26 16 18 152 1 152
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-04-02 8 0 8 52 0 52
Total weighted health score
127.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (34)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

33 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-11
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-11
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-11
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-11
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-11
0635 Provide doctor's orders for the resident's immediate care at the time the resident was admitted. D 2025-08-21 2025-09-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-08-21 2025-09-17
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-08-21 2025-09-17
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. G 2025-05-15 2025-06-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-05-15 2025-06-16
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-03-27 2025-04-29
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-02-12 2025-03-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-02-12 2025-03-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-02-12 2025-03-12
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-01-29 2025-02-19
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-12-10 2025-01-07
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-12-10 2025-01-07
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2024-12-10 2025-01-07
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-12-10 2025-01-07
